canonical_name: session context and hosted mutation runtime
summary: prism-mcp modules that hold per-session runtime context, execute authenticated hosted mutations, and shape follow-up runtime/read-model views such as task context and heartbeat guidance.
evidence: `session_state`, hosted mutation entrypoints, and runtime/read-model views still form the execution half of the MCP mutation path.
After the authority cutover, this layer keeps session context for handles, task context, and post-mutation guidance rather than acting as ambient coordination authority.
risk_hint: Breakage here causes authenticated mutations to lose session context, return misleading follow-up views, or surface stale heartbeat guidance.
